mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over the last month, the market reads an easier discount-rate path and stronger liquidity conditions, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if declining FEDFUNDS periods do not outperform the unconditional 20d return distribution, or if triggered cases show negative average 20d returns in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in CPIAUCSL inflation forces expectations of policy tightening, raises real-rate fears, and pressures equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above 3% and FEDFUNDS is below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation triggers are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the interaction signal has rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE signals weakening labor demand and rising recession risk, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE rising-threshold episodes do not deliver negative average 20d returns, or if the sign of the conditional return is positive in most triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE indicates broad labor-market deterioration, which should raise recession probability and reduce S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal fails to outperform either series alone, or if 5d forward SPX returns are not negative in a majority of joint-deterioration cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates stronger retail demand and household spending momentum, which supports corporate revenue expectations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month moving average is above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if retail-sales strength does not coincide with above-average 20d SPX returns, or if the signal loses predictive power after controlling for recent index level drift.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO with rising HOUST captures synchronized supply-side expansion in industry and housing, which should support earnings breadth and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both rise month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint improvement in industrial production and housing starts is not associated with positive 60d forward returns, or if either series alone explains the effect as well as the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth accelerates while RSAFS remains firm, the economy is expanding on both aggregate output and consumer demand, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth rises and RSAFS is above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if synchronized GDP and retail-strength episodes do not produce positive 60d returns, or if the interaction term is not materially better than each standalone proxy.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, high FEDFUNDS combined with elevated CPIAUCSL keeps real rates restrictive and compresses equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 2% and CPIAUCSL inflation is above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-plus-inflation episodes are not followed by negative 5d SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the conditional effect disappears in rolling 1-year tests.
